caseStudy.title: Feature Store for ML Platform
caseStudy.description: Centralised feature engineering to accelerate model development.
caseStudy.overview: A fintech company's data science teams were duplicating feature engineering work across projects, spending 60% of their time on data preparation rather than model development. Each team maintained its own feature pipelines with no sharing or reuse.
caseStudy.problem: Duplicated feature engineering led to subtle inconsistencies between models using nominally the same features. New model development required weeks of data preparation before experimentation could begin. Training-serving skew caused by different feature computation in training vs. production led to model performance degradation.
caseStudy.solution: A centralised feature store was built, housing over 500 production-grade features with consistent computation logic for both batch training and real-time serving. Feature discovery tooling allowed data scientists to browse and reuse existing features. Automated monitoring detected feature drift and training-serving skew in production.

stats.label: Time to first model experiment (down from 10)

results.summary: Data scientists now spend the majority of their time on model development rather than data preparation. The feature store has become the foundation of the company's ML platform, with new models benefiting from years of accumulated feature engineering work.
